things.name: Egyptian Museum
things.description: The world's second-greatest Egyptian antiquities museum after Cairo, with 30,000 artefacts including complete royal mummies

things.name: Mole Antonelliana
things.description: Turin's iconic 167-metre tower housing the National Cinema Museum, with a glass panoramic lift ascending to stunning views

things.name: Palazzo Reale
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage royal palace of the House of Savoy with lavish state apartments, armory and royal gardens

things.name: Piazza Castello
things.description: Turin's magnificent central square dominated by Palazzo Madama and Palazzo Reale at the heart of the baroque capital

things.name: Holy Shroud of Turin (Cathedral)
things.description: The San Giovanni Battista Cathedral houses the famous Shroud of Turin, Christendom's most debated and venerated relic

things.name: Juventus Stadium (Allianz Stadium)
things.description: Home of Juventus FC with a 41,500-seat capacity; the club's museum displays 36 Serie A titles and 14 Coppa Italia trophies

things.name: National Automobile Museum (MAUTO)
things.description: World-class automotive museum with 200 cars tracing 130 years of motoring history in Turin, birthplace of Fiat and Lancia

things.name: Porta Palazzo Market
things.description: Europe's largest open-air market selling everything from fresh produce to antiques in Turin's most vibrant neighbourhood

things.name: Galleria Sabauda
things.description: Royal picture gallery with Flemish, Dutch and Italian masterpieces including works by Van Eyck, Van Dyck and Mantegna

things.name: Parco del Valentino
things.description: Turin's grandest park along the Po with a French baroque castle, botanical gardens and a reconstructed medieval village
